skill.name: ux-researcher-designer
skill.description: UX research and design toolkit for persona generation, journey mapping, usability testing, research synthesis, and design validation.
skill.summary: Generate research-backed UX personas and design insights from structured user patterns.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many weak cryptography alerts, but review found no cryptographic code or malicious intent. The alerts are false positives from UX and data wording, while the only real risk surface is a local Python script documented for user execution.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static weak cryptography findings matched normal UX, design, device, and data-related words. No hash, cipher, crypto import, or secret handling code was found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain plain product, UX, or data terminology. Review found no cryptographic API usage or security-sensitive algorithm choice.

low_findings.title: Documented Local Script Invocation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md documents running python scripts/persona_generator.py. This is a local user-invoked command, and the script does not call subprocess, shell execution, network APIs, environment variables, or file writes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is documentation for a local Python script, not dynamic execution inside the skill. The script only selects output format and prints generated persona data.

content.user_title: Build UX Research Personas and Journey Insights
content.value_statement: Teams need reliable UX research outputs from scattered user data and interviews. This skill structures personas, journeys, test plans, and design implications for faster product decisions.
actual_capabilities: Generates research-backed persona profiles from structured user behavior data.
Classifies users into power user, casual user, business user, or mobile-first archetypes.
Extracts goals, frustrations, motivations, behaviors, and design implications.
Formats persona outputs for readable review by product and design teams.
Provides a UX research workflow covering journeys, usability tests, synthesis, and validation.
limitations: The script uses sample data by default and does not parse an external dataset file.
Persona classification uses simple heuristics and four predefined archetypes.
It does not replace participant recruitment, interviews, or direct usability testing.
Research conclusions require human review before product decisions.

use_cases.title: Create Product Personas
use_cases.description: Turn survey patterns and interview notes into clear personas with goals, behaviors, frustrations, and design implications.

use_cases.title: Plan Usability Studies
use_cases.description: Define testing goals, participant segments, success criteria, and synthesis questions before a moderated or unmoderated study.

use_cases.title: Align Roadmap Decisions
use_cases.description: Summarize user needs and journey pain points so product teams can prioritize evidence-based design improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize User Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize these user research notes into goals, frustrations, behaviors, and design implications. Keep the output concise and actionable.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Persona
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a UX persona from these survey responses and interview insights. Include archetype, demographics, motivations, needs, frustrations, scenarios, and confidence level.

prompt_templates.title: Map the Journey
prompt_templates.scenario: Journey mapping for a product flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a customer journey map for this workflow. Include stages, user actions, pain points, emotions, opportunities, and design recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Design Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this proposed design against the persona, journey map, and usability risks. Identify assumptions, evidence gaps, test tasks, and prioritised design changes.

output_examples.input: Survey data shows frequent dashboard use, mobile access, and repeated complaints about slow reporting.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a persona with a likely mobile-first or power-user archetype, key frustrations, usage scenarios, and design implications such as faster reporting and touch-friendly workflows.

output_examples.input: A product team needs to test a redesigned onboarding flow for business users.
output_examples.output: The skill outlines participant segments, usability tasks, success measures, likely pain points, and synthesis prompts for comparing findings against business-user needs.

output_examples.input: Interview notes mention collaboration problems, export needs, and unclear permissions.
output_examples.output: The skill summarizes themes into user goals, journey friction, design opportunities, and roadmap questions for team collaboration features.

best_practices: Provide real research notes, survey fields, and product context before asking for personas.
Review generated personas with stakeholders and validate them against observed user behavior.
Separate evidence, assumptions, and recommendations in final research deliverables.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated personas as proven research without participant evidence.
Do not use the sample data output as a final product persona.
Do not merge unrelated user segments into one persona for convenience.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create UX personas, journey maps, usability testing plans, research summaries, and design validation recommendations.

faq.question: Can it analyze real user research?
faq.answer: Yes, it can structure supplied research notes and user data. The included script uses sample data unless adapted.

faq.question: Does it conduct user interviews?
faq.answer: No. It organizes and synthesizes research inputs, but humans must recruit participants and run studies.

faq.question: What tools is it compatible with?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is the persona generator data-driven?
faq.answer: It uses structured user fields and interview insights to infer archetypes, goals, frustrations, and design implications.

faq.question: What should teams review before using outputs?
faq.answer: Teams should verify data quality, sample size, segment fit, and whether recommendations match observed user behavior.
